input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is hitting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference between VSTest and MTP.

In MTP, exit code 8 means "no tests were discovered/run." VSTest would return exit code 0 in this case. The fix is to use the `--ignore-exit-code` option or set the `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE` environment variable.

Let me look at their project to give context-specific advice.

data.content:
This is a known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8** means "zero tests discovered," which MTP treats as an error (VSTest returned 0).

**Fix:** Add this property to your `.csproj` (or `Directory.Build.props` for all projects):

```xml
<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
```

Alternatively, in CI you can pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` on the `dotnet test` command line.

Let me apply the project-level fix:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.content:
The fix works. With zero tests discovered (since `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` isn't defined), the process now exits with **code 0** instead of 8.

**Summary of the issue:**
- MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0)
- Adding `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` restores the VSTest behavior

If you have multiple test projects, put this in a shared `Directory.Build.props` so it applies everywhere.
